90/90 hip lift against wall
instructions:
Lightly dig heels down

Tuck tailbone slightly (flatten low back)

Exhale fully through mouth

Hold 4–5 breaths
Pull your tailbone toward your heels.

Chair Sit → Chest Press → Overhead Reach
instructions:
•	Sit down onto a chair slowly (controlled).
•	From seated: press dumbbells forward like a chest press.
•	Stand up and reach both dumbbells overhead.
Why: Legs + glutes + posture + shoulder mobility.

name: Cross legged practise
instructions: Hold for 30 - 60 seconds - breathe!

name: Cross-Back Toe Tap → Rotating Row
instructions:
•	Tap one foot behind you diagonally (curtsy-style tap).
•	As you tap, row the opposite dumbbell backward (rotation from ribs, not hips).
•	Alternate sides.
Why: Upper back + rotational core + safe hip mobility.

name: Forward Reach Step → Row → Stand Tall
instructions:
•	Step one foot forward like you’re reaching for something.
•	Lean slightly and perform a double-arm row.
•	Step back and lengthen your spine tall.
Why: Safe hinge training + posture + stepping mechanics.

name: Half kneeling hold with foot walk
Heel Lift → Hammer Curl → Slow Twist
instructions:
•	Lift heels into a gentle calf raise.
•	Do a hammer curl.
•	At the top, add a tiny controlled rib twist (like turning to look at something).
•	Lower slowly.
Why: Ankles + arms + core rotators.

March → Biceps Curl → Press Back
instructions:
•	March in place.
•	Curl the dumbbells.
•	At the top, push the dumbbells slightly behind you (like a triceps press-back).
•	Lower, keep marching.
Why: Arms + posture + light cardio.

name: Mini Squat → Front Raise → Side Step
instructions:
•	Do a small squat.
•	As you rise, lift the dumbbells to shoulder height (front raise).
•	Immediately step to the side, lower, reset.
Why: Legs + shoulders + stability.

•	Step to the side.

Why: Shoulders + upper back + coordination + balance.

name: Standing single leg RDL with banded row
Tall kneeling
instructions:
1. Tall kneeling hold — fingertips on bench · 3 × 5 sec
Kneel upright on thick mat, hips over knees. Squeeze glutes, tall spine. Monitor knee comfort — never force. This alone is the exercise on difficult days.

2. Tall kneeling — no hands · 3 × 10 sec
Hands to chest. Progress to eyes closed when steady. 

3. Tall kneeling weight shift · 8 reps
Slowly shift weight left to right. Don't let hips drop. Mimics lateral instability of water. One hand available if needed.

name: Tall kneeling to stand with DB hold
instructions: (hold at chest or sides)

name: The World's Greatest Stretch (Mobility Exercise) by Squat University
instructions:
Regression - hip & shoulder friendly 
Step 1: Setup

Start in a half-kneeling lunge (back knee on the floor, front foot outside the front hand).

Hands on the ground or on yoga blocks for extra support.

Step 2: Hip-Friendly Adjustment

Keep the front knee stacked over the ankle (don’t let it drive too far forward).

Keep the lunge moderate — no need for a deep split stance.

Step 3: Shoulder-Friendly Twist

Place the inside hand (same side as the front leg) on the floor for balance.

Slowly rotate your chest toward the front leg.

Instead of a big arm sweep overhead, you can:

Option 1: Place your hand on your chest as you rotate.

Option 2: Reach the arm out just to shoulder height (not overhead).

name: Wall lean with core breath with or without stability ball
instructions:
Hands at or above face level
Inhale, let belly relax, then exhale and draw belly up and in.  Focus on the lift while keeping spine neutral.
